diff_user.system:
You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Building. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: architecturalStyle - Example triple: (103 Colmore Row | architecturalStyle | Brutalist architecture)
Predicate: buildingStartDate - Example triple: (103 Colmore Row | buildingStartDate | "1973")
Predicate: completionDate - Example triple: (103 Colmore Row | completionDate | 1976)
Predicate: floorCount - Example triple: (103 Colmore Row | floorCount | 23)
Predicate: location - Example triple: (103 Colmore Row | location | "Colmore Row, Birmingham, England")
Predicate: cost - Example triple: (108 St Georges Terrace | cost | "120 million (Australian dollars)")
Predicate: floorArea - Example triple: (108 St Georges Terrace | floorArea | 39500.0 (square metres))
Predicate: architect - Example triple: (11 Diagonal Street | architect | Helmut Jahn)
Predicate: owner - Example triple: (11 Diagonal Street | owner | Redefine Properties Limited)
Predicate: formerName - Example triple: (320 South Boston Building | formerName | "Exchange National Bank Building")
Predicate: height - Example triple: (320 South Boston Building | height | 121.92 (metres))
Predicate: buildingType - Example triple: (3Arena | buildingType | "Concert and events venue")
Predicate: developer - Example triple: (AC Hotel Bella Sky Copenhagen | developer | Bella Center)
Predicate: tenant - Example triple: (AC Hotel Bella Sky Copenhagen | tenant | Marriott International)
Predicate: isPartOf - Example triple: (Adare | isPartOf | Munster)
Predicate: country - Example triple: (Addis Ababa | country | Ethiopia)
Predicate: currentTenants - Example triple: (Addis Ababa City Hall | currentTenants | "Government of Addis Ababa")
Predicate: address - Example triple: (Adisham Hall | address | "St. Benedict's Monastery, Adisham, Haputhale, Sri Lanka")
Predicate: inaugurationDate - Example triple: (Alan B. Miller Hall | inaugurationDate | "2009-10-02")
Predicate: birthPlace - Example triple: (Alfred Giles (architect) | birthPlace | England)
Predicate: deathPlace - Example triple: (Alfred Giles (architect) | deathPlace | Kendall County, Texas)
Predicate: bedCount - Example triple: (Ampara Hospital | bedCount | 476)
Predicate: region - Example triple: (Ampara Hospital | region | Ampara District)
Predicate: state - Example triple: (Ampara Hospital | state | Eastern Province, Sri Lanka)
Predicate: website - Example triple: (Ampara Hospital | website | <http://www.ghampara.gov.lk/>)
Predicate: yearOfConstruction - Example triple: (Asher and Mary Isabelle Richardson House | yearOfConstruction | 1911)
Predicate: NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber - Example triple: (Asilomar Conference Grounds | NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber | "87000823")
Predicate: addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces - Example triple: (Asilomar Conference Grounds | addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces | "1987-02-27")
Predicate: significantBuilding - Example triple: (Augustus Pugin | significantBuilding | Palace of Westminster)
Predicate: governingBody - Example triple: (Birmingham | governingBody | Birmingham City Council)
Predicate: leader - Example triple: (Birmingham | leader | Andrew Mitchell)
Predicate: chancellor - Example triple: (College of William & Mary | chancellor | Robert Gates)
Predicate: governmentType - Example triple: (County Limerick | governmentType | Limerick City and County Council)
Predicate: capital - Example triple: (Denmark | capital | Copenhagen)
Predicate: language - Example triple: (Denmark | language | Faroese language)
Predicate: leaderTitle - Example triple: (Dublin | leaderTitle | Dáil Éireann)
Predicate: currency - Example triple: (Ethiopia | currency | Ethiopian birr)
Predicate: ethnicGroup - Example triple: (Japan | ethnicGroup | Chinese people in Japan)
Predicate: origin - Example triple: (John Madin | origin | Birmingham)
Predicate: significantProject - Example triple: (Julia Morgan | significantProject | Hearst Castle)
Predicate: foundationPlace - Example triple: (Marriott International | foundationPlace | Washington, D.C.)
Predicate: demonym - Example triple: (Republic of Ireland | demonym | Irish people)
Predicate: numberOfRooms - Example triple: (AC Hotel Bella Sky Copenhagen | numberOfRooms | 814)
Predicate: keyPerson - Example triple: (Marriott International | keyPerson | Bill Marriott)
Predicate: architecture - Example triple: (Asilomar Conference Grounds | architecture | "Arts and Crafts Movement and American craftsman Bungalows")
Predicate: postalCode - Example triple: (Birmingham | postalCode | B postcode area)

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a building into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Build a map of subject -> triples and collect all entities
    subject_triples = {}
    all_subjects = set()
    all_objects = set()

    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[t.subject] = []
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)
        all_subjects.add(t.subject)
        all_objects.add(t.object)

    # Find the main subject (most triples about one subject)
    main_subject = max(subject_triples.keys(), key=lambda s: len(subject_triples[s]))
    main_triples = subject_triples[main_subject]

    # Build sentence parts with proper grammar
    sentence_parts = []

    # Start with main building name
    sentence_parts.append(main_subject)

    # Clean object values (remove surrounding quotes if present)
    def clean_value(val):
        if val.startswith('"') and val.endswith('"'):
            return val[1:-1]
        if val.startswith("'") and val.endswith("'"):
            return val[1:-1]
        return val

    # Collect information by category
    architect_info = None
    style_info = None
    location_info = []
    construction_info = []
    type_info = None
    owner_info = None
    other_info = []

    for t in main_triples:
        clean_obj = clean_value(t.object)
        if t.predicate == "architect":
            architect_info = clean_obj
        elif t.predicate == "architecturalStyle":
            style_info = clean_obj
        elif t.predicate == "buildingType":
            type_info = clean_obj
        elif t.predicate == "owner":
            owner_info = clean_obj
        elif t.predicate == "location":
            location_info.append(f"located at {clean_obj}")
        elif t.predicate == "country":
            location_info.append(f"in {clean_obj}")
        elif t.predicate == "state":
            location_info.append(f"in {clean_obj}")
        elif t.predicate == "region":
            location_info.append(f"in the region of {clean_obj}")
        elif t.predicate == "address":
            location_info.append(f"at {clean_obj}")
        elif t.predicate == "isPartOf":
            location_info.append(f"part of {clean_obj}")
        elif t.predicate == "height":
            construction_info.append(f"{clean_obj} metres tall")
        elif t.predicate == "floorCount":
            construction_info.append(f"{clean_obj} floors")
        elif t.predicate == "cost":
            construction_info.append(f"cost {clean_obj}")
        elif t.predicate == "floorArea":
            construction_info.append(f"{clean_obj} floor area")
        elif t.predicate in ["buildingStartDate", "yearOfConstruction"]:
            construction_info.append(f"built in {clean_obj}")
        elif t.predicate == "completionDate":
            construction_info.append(f"completed in {clean_obj}")
        elif t.predicate == "inaugurationDate":
            construction_info.append(f"inaugurated on {clean_obj}")
        elif t.predicate in ["numberOfRooms", "bedCount", "tenant", "currentTenants", "developer"]:
            other_info.append(t)

    # Build the sentence with proper connectors
    result_parts = []

    # Add type if available
    if type_info:
        result_parts.append(f"{main_subject} is a {type_info}")
    else:
        result_parts.append(main_subject)

    # Add type of building if type exists
    if type_info:
        pass  # Already handled above

    # Add architect and style info
    if architect_info or style_info:
        design_parts = []
        if architect_info:
            design_parts.append(f"designed by {architect_info}")
        if style_info:
            design_parts.append(f"in {style_info} style")
        if design_parts:
            result_parts.append(f", {', '.join(design_parts)}")

    # Add owner if available
    if owner_info:
        result_parts.append(f", owned by {owner_info}")

    # Add location info
    if location_info:
        result_parts.append(f", {', '.join(location_info)}")

    # Add construction info
    if construction_info:
        result_parts.append(f", with {' and '.join(construction_info)}")

    # Add other info
    for t in other_info:
        if t.predicate == "numberOfRooms":
            result_parts.append(f"containing {t.object} rooms")
        elif t.predicate == "bedCount":
            result_parts.append(f"with {t.object} beds")
        elif t.predicate == "tenant":
            result_parts.append(f"tenant is {t.object}")
        elif t.predicate == "currentTenants":
            result_parts.append(f"current tenants include {t.object}")
        elif t.predicate == "developer":
            result_parts.append(f"developed by {t.object}")

    # Join all parts
    sentence = ''.join(result_parts)

    # Handle other subjects in triples (like country relationships)
    other_subject_triples = [t for t in triples if t.subject != main_subject]
    if other_subject_triples:
        related_clauses = []
        for t in other_subject_triples:
            if t.predicate == "capital":
                related_clauses.append(f"where the capital is {t.object}")
            elif t.predicate == "leader":
                related_clauses.append(f"led by {t.object}")
            elif t.predicate == "country":
                related_clauses.append(f"which is in {t.object}")

        if related_clauses:
            sentence += f", {', '.join(related_clauses)}"

    # Ensure proper capitalization and punctuation
    sentence = sentence.strip()
    if sentence:
        sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
        if not sentence.endswith('.'):
            sentence += '.'

    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```
